steps.title: Step 1
steps.content: If $x + 28y$ is a factor of an expression, then setting $x = -28y$ must make the expression equal zero (factor theorem).

steps.title: Step 2
steps.content: Substitute $x = -28y$ into each answer choice and check for zero.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.content:
• Choice A — $x^{2}+29xy+28y^{2}$: Substitute $x=-28y$: $784y^2 - 812y^2 + 28y^2 = 0$, so $x+28y$ is a factor. ✓
  • Choice B — $x^{2}+29xy+29y^{2}$: Substitute $x=-28y$: $784y^2 - 812y^2 + 29y^2 = y^2$, not zero. ✗
  • Choice C — $x^{2}+84xy+84y^{2}$: Substitute $x=-28y$: $784y^2 - 2352y^2 + 84y^2 = -1484y^2$, not zero. ✗
  • Choice D — $x^{2}+58xy+87y^{2}$: Substitute $x=-28y$: $784y^2 - 1624y^2 + 87y^2 = -753y^2$, not zero. ✗
That confirms A is correct.

steps.title: Step 4
steps.content: Those choices, and D yield $y^2$, $-1484y^2$, and $-753y^2$ respectively — none equal zero.

eliminationHtml:
• Choice A — $x^{2}+29xy+28y^{2}$: Substitute $x=-28y$: $784y^2 - 812y^2 + 28y^2 = 0$. Zero confirms $x+28y$ is a factor. ✓
  • Choice B — $x^{2}+29xy+29y^{2}$: Substitute $x=-28y$: $784y^2 - 812y^2 + 29y^2 = y^2 eq 0$. ✗
  • Choice C — $x^{2}+84xy+84y^{2}$: Substitute $x=-28y$: $784y^2 - 2352y^2 + 84y^2 = -1484y^2 eq 0$. ✗
  • Choice D — $x^{2}+58xy+87y^{2}$: Substitute $x=-28y$: $784y^2 - 1624y^2 + 87y^2 = -753y^2 eq 0$. ✗
That confirms A is correct.
